apaka-.function: alternative form of napaka- Apakabobo naman ng mga kamoteng 'to.These dumb drivers are so stupid.

anti-.etymology: Borrowed from Spanish anti-, from Ancient Greek ἀντι- (anti-).

de-.function: prepositional particle added to indicate possession of quality or object de- + baterya (“battery”) → de-baterya (“with battery; powered by battery”) de- + gulong (“wheel”) → de-gulong (“with wheels”) de- + kahoy (“wood”) → de-kahoy (“wooden”) de- + koryente (“electricity”) → de-koryente (“electrical”) de- + kolor (“color”) → de-kolor (“colored”)

dagi-.function: used to form nouns related to electricity: electro- Synonym: elektro- dagi- + haynayan (“biology”) → dagihaynayan (“electrobiology”) dagi- + lusaw (“liquid”) → dagilusaw (“electrolyte”)

danum-.etymology: From Kapampangan danum, from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *danum, from Proto-Austronesian *daNum.
danum-.function: used to form nouns related to water: hydro- danum- + sigwasan (“mechanics”) → danumsigwasan (“hydrodynamics”) danum- + haynayan (“biology”) → danumhaynayan (“hydrobiology”)

dalub-.etymology: Clipping of dalubhasa, from Malay jurubahasa, on the pattern of Malay juru-, from Javanese ꦗꦸꦫꦸ (juru), from Old Javanese juru.
dalub-.function: to form nouns denoting a person who studies a particular discipline: -ist, -ian dalub- + wika (“language”) → dalubwika (“linguist”) dalub- + hayop (“animal”) → dalubhayop (“zoologist”); to make a person of something related to a much higher degree dalub- + guro (“teacher”) → dalubguro (“professor”)

a-.etymology: Borrowed from Spanish a, from Latin ad.
a-.function: at (indicating time) Puntahan kita sa a-primero ng Marso.I'll go to you at the first of March. Sahuran tuwing a-kinse ng bawat buwan.It is payday every 15th of every month. A-treynta y uno kahapon.Yesterday was the 31st.; Only used before Spanish cardinal numbers to tell the date for a month. For the first day of a month, a-primero is more correct but a-uno is also used by younger speakers. The prefix has the same function as ika- for Tagalog cardinal numbers.; The prefix is optional but Spanish-oriented speakers often use it.; forms contemplative aspect forms for verbs in the object or directional trigger; Can be considered as a dialectal equivalent to the reduplicated syllable for some verbs. á- + tápon (“throw”) → átápon (“will be thrown”): equivalent to itatapon á- + inóm (“drink”) + -in → áinumín (“will be drunk”): equivalent to iinumin á- + gawâ (“drink”) + -an → ágaw-án (“will be done for”): equivalent to gagawan á- + pakuluan (“to be boiled”) → ápakuluán (“will let to be boiled”): equivalent to pakukuluan; When used with in-, forms the progressive aspect forms of verbs. in- + á- + tápon (“throw”) → inátápon (“being thrown”): equivalent to itinatapon in- + á- + inóm (“drink”) → ináinóm (“being drunk”): equivalent to iniinom in- + á- + gawâ (“drink”) + -an → inágaw-án (“being done for”): equivalent to ginagawan in- + á- + pakuluan (“to be let boiled”) → inápakuluán (“letting to be boiled”): equivalent to pinakukuluan; See also[edit]; ga-maga-naga-

ga-.function: used to indicate of a similar extremeness in size: as big as; as large as; as small as; as little as; as tiny as; ga- + bahay (“house”) → gabahay (“as big as a house”) ga- + bundok (“mountain”) → gabundok (“as big as a mountain”) ga- + bunton (“stack”) → gabunton (“as tall as a stack”) ga- + kulangot (“booger”) → gakulangot (“as small as a booger”) ga- + tulo (“drop”) → gatulo (“as little as a drop”) ga- + butil (“grain”) → gabutil (“as tiny as a grain”); used to indicate similarity: like; similar to ga- + nito (“this”) → ganito (“like this”) ga- + niyan (“that”) → ganiyan (“like that”)

ga-.etymology: Shortened form of naga-. Compare nag-.
ga-.function: ga- (contemplative maga-, mag-, imperative pag-); alternative form of nag-, naga- Galuto ko og utan ― I am cooking vegetables; Commonly used, as does nag-, in contrast with naga- which is only ever used in formal situations or literature.; See usage notes for nag- and naga-.; Related terms[edit]; gi-gina-ging-

gaga-.function: used to indicate of a similarly small size: as small as; as tiny as gaga- + butil (“grain”) → gagabutil (“as small as a grain”); See also[edit]; ga-; kaka-; Anagrams[edit]; agag, ag-ag

git-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
gi- — for roots starting in letter ⟨t⟩
From clipping of gitna.
git-.function: used to form nouns denoting its position in the middle or center git- + lapi (“affix”) → gitlapi (“infix”) git- + tuldok (“period, dot, point”) → gituldok (“middle dot”) git- + lawas (“corpse, body”) → gitlawas (“centrosome”)

gi-.function: alternative form of git-; Used for roots starting in letter ⟨t⟩.

gi-.etymology: Apparently from an older form gina-. Compare Hiligaynon gina- and dialectal Tagalog ina- which also mark the perfective aspect in non-agent triggers.
gi-.function: forms the complete aspect in all triggers except the active trigger Gikaon nako ang mangga (patient trigger)I ate the mango (mango is focused) Giadtoan nako ang merkado (locative trigger)We went to the market (market is focused); References[edit]; John U. Wolff (1972), A dictionary of Cebuano Visayan[1] (overall work in Cebuano and English), Ithaca, NY: Cornell University Press

hing-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
hi-, hin-, him-
From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *hin-, from Proto-Austronesian *Sin-.
hing-.function: prefix indicating removal or cleaning hing- + kuto (“louse”) → hinguto (“to remove lice (from hair or fur)”); prefix indicating state or condition hi- + ganti (“return, reward”) → higanti (“revenge”) him- + pasok (“to enter, go in”) → himasok (“to meddle, interfere”) him- + patay (“dead, to kill”) → himatay (“to lose consciousness, faint”) hin- + sakit (“sickness, pain”) (with CV- reduplication) → hinanakit (“grudge, ill will”)

hing-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
hing-
denotes inclination, habit or propensity

i-.function: benefactive trigger: to perform the action of the verb for someone or something (expresses various kinds of actions) i- + bili (“buy”) → ibili (“to buy something for someone”) Ibili mo ako ng saging.Buy me bananas.; object trigger: to do something to a person or a thing (expresses various kinds of actions) i- + tapon (“throw”) → itapon (“to throw”) Itapon mo iyan sa basurahan.Throw that to the garbage.; instrumental trigger: to use something for a certain purpose (expresses various kinds of actions) i- + sulat (“write”) → isulat (“to use something for writing”) Isulat mo ng listahan ang lapis.Use the pencil to write a list.

ika-.function: used to form ordinal numbers ika- + dalawa (“two”) → ikalawa (“second”); used to express o'clock Synonym: alas ika- + lima (“five”) → ikalima (“five o'clock”); Tagalog ordinal numbers prefixed with ika- are usually more formal compared to those prefixed with pang-, but ika- is also preferred in temporal rankings.; used to form verbs with causative focus ika- + galit (“anger”) → ikagalit (“to cause one's anger”) ika- + galak (“delight”) → ikagalak (“to cause one's gladness”)

ika-; Used to form ordinal numbers.; Attached to a numerical symbol to denote an ordinal number. 7 (pito) ― ika-7 (ika-pito); The ordinal is linked to a noun with the use of the linker "nga"(short form: "-ng") or alternatively, but less frequently, with "ka". Ikalima nga adlaw / Ikalimang adlaw / Ikalima ka adlaw ― Fifth day

ikapag-.etymology:
A user has added this entry to requests for verification(+) If it cannot be verified that this term meets our attestation criteria, it will be deleted. Feel free to edit this entry as normal, but do not remove {{rfv}} until the request has been resolved.
From ika- + pag-.
ikapag-.function: used to form verbs with causative focus, denoting the reason for doing something (for mag- verbs) ikapag- + aral (“study”) → ikapag-aral (“to be the reason for studying”) (from mag-aral) ikapag- + hirap (“poor”) → ikapaghirap (“to be the reason for becoming poor”) (from maghirap)

ipa-.function: (causative) to have or ask something done ipa- + gawa (“to do”) → ipagawa (“to have someone do something”) Ipagawa mo 'yan sa kanya.Have him do that; (accusative) to do something to something or someone ipa- + malas (“to show”) → ipamalas (“to show”); (accusative) to make a slightly reproachful or supplicative request to have done something unattended or done improperly ipa- + luto-luto (“to cook”) → ipaluto-luto (“to have cooked a bit more”)

ipag-.function: to use as means or an instrument in doing a thing ipag- + ahit (“to shave”) → ipag-ahit (“to use for shaving”); to perform the action of the verb for someone ipag- + luto (“to cook”) → ipagluto (“to cook something for someone”); to perform the action of the verb to something with intensity ipag- + laban (“to fight”) → ipaglaban (“to fight for”) ipag- + tapat (“to confess”) → ipagtapat (“to confess something to somebody”); to be the cause or reason of ipag- + malaki (“big”) → ipagmalaki (“to be proud of, to be the cause of one's pride”) ipag- + hinakit (“to confess”) → ipaghihinanakit (“to confess something to somebody”)

in-.function: prefix form of -in-; Used when the root begins with a vowel. Though appearing to be a prefix, the affix actually still functions as an infix, inserted between the unwritten initial glottal stop and the root's initial vowel.

ipang-.function: Instrumental trigger - to do something using ipang- + luto (“cooking, cuisine”) → ipanluto (“to use something for cooking”) ipang- + punas (“wiping”) → ipampunas (“to use something for wiping”); Object trigger - to do something to a person or a thing ipang- + anak (“son, daughter”) → ipanganak (“to give birth to”) ipang- + ako (“promise”) → ipangako (“to promise”)

isa-.function: prefix that indicates to be an object of transfer of location or state

ka-.function: implies companionship or partnership ka- + sama (“to accompany”) → kasama (“partner”) ka- + opisina (“office”) → kaopisina (“coworker”); implies similarity or interaction ka- + mukha (“face”) → kamukha (“someone/something similar; look-alike”) ka- + away (“fight”) → kaaway (“enemy”); implies past time ka- + hapon (“afternoon”) → kahapon (“yesterday”) ka- + gabi (“night”) → kagabi (“last night”); (with initial syllable repetition) implies repeated action ka- + sigaw (“shout”) → kasisigaw (“repeatedly shouting”) ka- + isip (“mind, thought”) → kaiisip (“thinking a lot”); (with initial syllable repetition, followed by lang or lamang) implies recent action ka- + punta (“going”) → kapupunta (“recently went”) ka- + luto (“cooking, cuisine”) → kaluluto (“recently cooked”); (with initial two syllables repetition) implies causing or producing something in an extreme degree ka- + galang (“respect”) → kagalang-galang (“inspiring great respect”) ka- + paniwala (“belief”) → kapani-paniwala (“very plausible”)

(added to adjectives) denotes a state of being; -ness Synonyms: pagka-, ka- -an ka- + linaw (“calm”) → kalinaw (“calmness”) ka- + bugnaw (“cold”) → kabugnaw (“coldness”); (added to an adjective) used in exclamations; how... Synonyms: -a, ka- -a ka- + init (“hot”) → kainit! (“how hot!”); (added to nouns or verbs) denotes partnership or companionship; co- Synonym: isigka- ka- + klase (“class”) → kaklase (“classmate”) ka- + away (“to fight”) → kaaway (“enemy”); short for maka-

labing-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
labin- — for compounds with next stem starting with the letter ⟨d⟩, ⟨l⟩, ⟨s⟩, or ⟨t⟩.
labim- — for compounds with next stem starting with the letter ⟨b⟩ or ⟨p⟩.
From labi (“excess”) + -ng- (compound enclitic interfix). labing-isa would literally mean 1 excess from 10, with 10 implied.
labing-.function: Cardinal prefix, denoting excess over the count of ten, if the next stem in the compound starts with a vowel or ⟨w⟩. labing- + isa (“one”) → labing-isa (“eleven”) labing- + apat (“four”) → labing-apat (“fourteen”) labing- + anim (“six”) → labing-anim (“sixteen”) labing- + walo (“eight”) → labingwalo (“eighteen”)

ma-.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *ma-, from Proto-Austronesian *ma- (stative prefix). See also may (existential marker).
ma-.function: used to form adjectives indicating a quality ma- + linis (“cleanliness”) → malinis (“clean”); (actor III trigger prefix): infinitive form ma- + ligo (“bath”) → maligo (“to take a bath; to bathe”); (object IV trigger prefix): infinitive form ma- + basa (“wet”) → mabasa (“to become wet”) to have done something; to be able to do something ma- + tapos (“finish”) → matapos (“to be able to finish something”) Natapos kong basahin ang libro kagabi. ― I was able to finish reading the book last night. to perform the action of the verb unintentionally Coordinate term: -in ma- + basag (“break, crack”) → mabasag (“to break something unintentionally”) Nabasag ko ang salamin. ― I broke the mirror (unintentionally); to have done something; to be able to do something ma- + tapos (“finish”) → matapos (“to be able to finish something”) Natapos kong basahin ang libro kagabi. ― I was able to finish reading the book last night.; to perform the action of the verb unintentionally Coordinate term: -in ma- + basag (“break, crack”) → mabasag (“to break something unintentionally”) Nabasag ko ang salamin. ― I broke the mirror (unintentionally); Using ma- implies that the action performed is unintentional, while -in is used when the action was done on purpose: basag (“break, crack”) + -in → basagin (“to break something on purpose”) Binasag ko ang salamin. ― I broke the mirror (on purpose); used to form contemplative aspects of verbs prefixed with um- or infixed with -um-; See also[edit]; na-; Further reading[edit]; Blust, Robert; Trussel, Stephen; et al. (2023) “*ma-”, in the CLDF dataset from The Austronesian Comparative Dictionary (2010–), →DOI; Anagrams[edit]; am
Used to form adjectives indicating a quality; Used to form the future tense of a verb

kaka-.function: prefix denoting repeated action kaka- + sigaw (“shout”) → kakasigaw (“repeatedly shouting”) kaka- + isip (“mind, thought”) → kakaisip (“thinking a lot”); prefix denoting the recently completed aspect kaka- + punta (“going”) → kakapunta (“recently went”) kaka- + luto (“cooking, cuisine”) → kakaluto (“recently cooked”)

magka-.function: prefix for verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to occur involuntarily or unexpectedly magka- + salubong (“encounter”) → magkasalubong (“to meet unexpectedly”); prefix for verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to come to have or possess magka- + kotse (“car”) → magkakotse (“to get a car”)

kasing-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
kasim- — before initials ⟨b⟩ and ⟨p⟩
kasin- — before initials ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩, ⟨l⟩ and ⟨t⟩
From ka- + sing-.
kasing-.function: alternative form of sing-; Anagrams[edit]; sangki, sikang

kawalang-.function: (added to nouns) lack or absence of something

magkaka-.function: progressive form of magka- prefix for contemplative aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: will occur involuntarily or unexpectedly magkaka- + salubong (“encounter”) → magkakasalubong (“will meet unexpectedly”) prefix for contemplative aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: will come to have or possess magkaka- + kotse (“car”) → magkakakotse (“will get a car”); prefix for contemplative aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: will occur involuntarily or unexpectedly magkaka- + salubong (“encounter”) → magkakasalubong (“will meet unexpectedly”); prefix for contemplative aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: will come to have or possess magkaka- + kotse (“car”) → magkakakotse (“will get a car”)

laban-.function: anti-; References[edit]; Lupon sa Agham (1969), Gonsalo del Rosario, editor, Maugnaying Talasalitaang Pang-agham : Ingles-Pilipino [Correlative Word List for Sciences : English-Filipino] (overall work in English and Tagalog), Manila: National Book Store, Inc., →LCCN, →OL

magpa-.function: Actor trigger - to ask someone do something magpa- + laba (“laundry”) → magpalaba (“to ask someone do the laundry”) magpa- + pintura (“paint”) → magpapintura (“to ask someone do the painting”); Actor trigger - to do something for oneself magpa- + gupit (“cut”) → magpagupit (“to get a haircut”) magpa- + tuli (“circumcision”) → magpatuli (“to get circumcised”) magpa- + kasal (“wedding”) → magpakasal (“to get married”) magpa- + English checkup (“check up”) → magpa-checkup (“to get a check up”)

mag-.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Austronesian *maʀ-. Compare Malay ber-.
mag-.function: actor trigger verb prefix, used to express: an action Magluluto ako ng pagkain. ― I will cook food. the taking up of an occupation Nagplanong magdoktor si Mary noong 1987. ― Mary planned to become a doctor in 1987. the use or wearing of something Hindi siya marunong mag-Ingles. ― He doesn't know how to speak (use) English. Mag-amerikana ka at malamig sa labas. ― Wear a coat because it's cold outside. a reciprocal action (for some verbs) Mag-usap muna tayo. ― Let's talk to each other first. (with CV reduplication): an action that will be done repeatedly, continually, or intensely Magsisisigaw ang mga bata kapag ginawa mo iyan. ― The children would be screaming so much once you do that. (with root word reduplication): a reciprocal action involving three or more doers Magkikita-kita tayo sa susunod na linggo. ― We will see one another next week. (with root word reduplication): an action to be done occasionally, at random, a little, a bit, now and then, or here and there Maglakad-lakad si Pedro sa mall. ― Pedro will walk a bit in the mall.; an action Magluluto ako ng pagkain. ― I will cook food.; the taking up of an occupation Nagplanong magdoktor si Mary noong 1987. ― Mary planned to become a doctor in 1987.; the use or wearing of something Hindi siya marunong mag-Ingles. ― He doesn't know how to speak (use) English. Mag-amerikana ka at malamig sa labas. ― Wear a coat because it's cold outside.; a reciprocal action (for some verbs) Mag-usap muna tayo. ― Let's talk to each other first.; (with CV reduplication): an action that will be done repeatedly, continually, or intensely Magsisisigaw ang mga bata kapag ginawa mo iyan. ― The children would be screaming so much once you do that.; (with root word reduplication): a reciprocal action involving three or more doers Magkikita-kita tayo sa susunod na linggo. ― We will see one another next week.; (with root word reduplication): an action to be done occasionally, at random, a little, a bit, now and then, or here and there Maglakad-lakad si Pedro sa mall. ― Pedro will walk a bit in the mall.; verb prefix with no trigger/focus used to express being, occurrence, or existence Magpa-Pasko na bukas! ― It will be Christmas tomorrow! Maghahating-gabi na. ― It is almost midnight.; noun prefix used to indicate: relationship between two persons mag- + ama (“father”) → mag-ama (“father and child”) mag- + asawa (“spouse”) → mag-asawa (“married couple”) (with CV reduplication): relationship among three or more persons mag- + CV- + ina (“mother”) → mag-iina (“mother and children”) mag- + CV- + kapatid (“sibling”) → magkakapatid (“brothers and sisters, siblings”) (with CV reduplication): occupation mag- + CV- + saka (“farming”) → magsasaka (“farmer”) mag- + CV- + nakaw (“stealing”) → magnanakaw (“thief, robber”); relationship between two persons mag- + ama (“father”) → mag-ama (“father and child”) mag- + asawa (“spouse”) → mag-asawa (“married couple”); (with CV reduplication): relationship among three or more persons mag- + CV- + ina (“mother”) → mag-iina (“mother and children”) mag- + CV- + kapatid (“sibling”) → magkakapatid (“brothers and sisters, siblings”); (with CV reduplication): occupation mag- + CV- + saka (“farming”) → magsasaka (“farmer”) mag- + CV- + nakaw (“stealing”) → magnanakaw (“thief, robber”); If the root word begins with a vowel, insert a hyphen after the prefix.; mag- + aral → mag-aral; If the root word is capitalized, the root is written together with the suffix and in lowercase. If the capitalized root word starts with a vowel, the root may be kept in uppercase.; mag- + Pasko → magpasko mag- + Ingles → mag-ingles or mag-Ingles; Also see usage notes at -um-.

mag-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
maga- — formal, literary
Ultimately from Proto-Austronesian *maʀ-. See Tagalog mag-.
mag-.function: mag- (verb-forming suffix, inchoative naga-, nag-, ga-, contemplative maga-, mag-, imperative paga-, pag-); actor trigger verb prefix, infinitive form durative action, action or state continuing in time; see Imperfective aspect Coordinate term: mo- — non-durative agent trigger Magluto sila arong orasa. ― They cook food at this time action not necessarily intentional Wala siya magsulti ― He didn't say do something to oneself Gusto ko mag-ilis ― I want to change my clothes to become something; take an occupation Magdoktor siya ― He/she will become a doctor; durative action, action or state continuing in time; see Imperfective aspect Coordinate term: mo- — non-durative agent trigger Magluto sila arong orasa. ― They cook food at this time; action not necessarily intentional Wala siya magsulti ― He didn't say; do something to oneself Gusto ko mag-ilis ― I want to change my clothes; to become something; take an occupation Magdoktor siya ― He/she will become a doctor; contemplative aspect of mag- Magluto ko ― I will cook; mag- (noun-forming suffix); (with partial (CV) reduplication) forms agent nouns, indicating one who does on a regular basis; -er Synonyms: tig-, mangCV-, mang- -ay, mangCV- -ay, mang- -Vl- mag- + CV- + balaod (“law”) → magbabalaod (“lawmaker”) mag- + CV- + sibya (“broadcast”) → magsisibya (“radio broadcaster”) mag- + CV- + kulit (“carve”) → magkukulit (“sculptor”); (uncommon, with -Vl- insertion) forms agent nouns, same as above; -er mag- + -Vl- + bantay (“keep watch”) → magbalantay (“watchman”) mag- + -Vl- + himo (“create”) → maghilimo (“producer”); If the root begins with a vowel, a hyphen is inserted after the prefix.; mag- + ulan → mag-ulan; If the root is capitalized, the root is usually written together with the prefix and in lowercase. If the capitalized root starts with a vowel, it may be kept.; mag- + Pasko → magpasko mag- + Ingles → mag-ingles or mag-Ingles; See also[edit]; -an-onpaga- -anpaga- -on

magpaka-.function: to strive to be or do something magpaka- + saya (“joy”) → magpakasaya (“to strive to be happy”) magpaka- + patay (“death”) → magpakamatay (“to commit suicide”)

magsi-.function: to do something collectively magsi- + kain (“eating”) → magsikain (“to all eat, to eat collectively”) magsi- + balik (“return”) → magsibalik (“to all return, to return collectively”)

maka-.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *maka- (“potential action; ability to do something”).
maka-.function: Actor trigger refers to things that have been done unintentionally maka- + basag (“to break”) → makabasag (“break something unintentionally”) Nakabasag ang anak ko sa mall. ― My son broke something in the mall (unintentionally).; Object trigger when paired with kailangan (“necessary”), it usually refers to actions that have to be done or performed Kailangan mong makatapos ng pag-aaral. ― You have to finish your studies.; Actor trigger refers to things that happen by chance maka- + kita (“see”) → makakita (“to happen to see something”) Nakakita ako ng artista nung nasa mall ako. ― I happened to see an actor while I was in the mall.; being in favor of or being inclined towards something Bakit ka maka-Amerika? ― Why are you in favor of America?

used to refer to the action that the actor can perform; used to refer to an action that had an opportunity to be performed (noun prefix) used to refer to managing to get the referred root; (noun prefix) used to refer to managing to get the referred root; used to refer to accidental or unintentional action; (with initial syllable repetition) used to form adjectives caused with great extent; used to indicate the number of occurrence or repetition

mang-.etymology: Inherited from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *maN-. Compare Malay meng-.
mang-.function: 3rd actor trigger verb prefix to practice the action of the root mang- + gamot (“medicine”) → manggamot (“to practice medicine; to work as a doctor”) mang- + anak (“child”) → manganak (“to give birth”) mang- + bili (“buying”) → mamili (“to go shopping”) mang- + abala (“bothersome”) → mang-abala (“to bother”) mang- + kabayo (“horse”) → mangabayo (“to go horseback riding”) To take the state expressed by the root partially or temporarily mang- + pula (“red”) → mamula (“to redden; to go reddish”); to practice the action of the root mang- + gamot (“medicine”) → manggamot (“to practice medicine; to work as a doctor”) mang- + anak (“child”) → manganak (“to give birth”) mang- + bili (“buying”) → mamili (“to go shopping”) mang- + abala (“bothersome”) → mang-abala (“to bother”) mang- + kabayo (“horse”) → mangabayo (“to go horseback riding”); To take the state expressed by the root partially or temporarily mang- + pula (“red”) → mamula (“to redden; to go reddish”); (with root reduplication) Forms nouns signifying occupations or doer of the action expressed by the root mang- + isda (“fish”) → mangingisda (“fisher; fisherman”) mang- + batas (“law”) → mambabatas (“lawmaker; legislator”); Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.; The prefix becomes mam- before ⟨b⟩ or ⟨p⟩. Where the root begins with ⟨p⟩, the consonants in question may be deleted.; the prefix becomes man- before ⟨d⟩, ⟨l⟩, ⟨s⟩, ⟨t⟩. Where the root begins with ⟨s⟩ or ⟨t⟩, the consonants in question may be deleted (e.g. manusok, root tusok).; Where the initial of the root is a /k/, the sound is dropped (e.g. manguha, root kuha).; Where the initial of the root is a vowel (starting with a glottal stop), the resulting affixed form is written without a hyphen (e.g. manganak, root anak), however this is no longer observed in more recent affixed forms where a hyphen is added after the suffix (e.g. mang-inis instead of manginis, root inis).

mang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
mam-
man-
mang- -ay — regional, with initial syllable repetition
From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *maN-. Compare Malay meng-.
mang-.function: added to bases with prefix pang- to indicate future or subjunctive volitional active forms; (with initial syllable repetition) forms nouns signifying occupations or doer of the action expressed by the root; Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.

mai-.function: used to form verbs that indicates capability to do something; used only in i- prefixed verbs mai- + balik (“return”) → maibalik (“to be able to return something”) mai- + sugod (“rush”) → maisugod (“to be able to rush something or someone”) mai- + tama (“correct”) → maitama (“to be able to correct something or someone”); Related terms[edit]; nai-

mapag-.function: used to form adjectives denoting having the habit or prone to mapag- + mahal (“love”) → mapagmahal (“loving”) mapag- + aruga (“care”) → mapag-aruga (“caring”); used to form adjectives denoting having the habit of trying to be (before adjectives formed by ma-) mapag- + magaling (“excellent”) → mapagmagaling (“showing off as being excellent; vainglorious”) mapag- + mataas (“high”) → mapagmataas (“showing off as being high; arrogant”)

makipag-.function: actor trigger - indicates engagement of the action of the verb with someone makipag- + bati (“reconciled”) → makipagbati (“to reconcile with someone”) makipag- + kaibigan (“friend”) → makipagkaibigan (“to make friends with someone”) makipag- + away (“quarrel”) → makipag-away (“to quarrel with someone”) makipag- + chat (“chat”) → makipagchat (“to chat with someone”) makipag- + talik (“sexual intercourse”) → makipagtalik (“to have sex with someone”) makipag- + usap (“talk”) → makipag-usap (“to talk to someone”)

mapa-.function: to do something unintentionally; to happen to do something mapa- + hiya (“crying”) → mapahiya (“to cry unintentionally”) mapa- + daan (“pass by”) → mapadaan (“to happen to pass by”); to be able to get someone do something; to be able to have someone do something mapa- + kain (“eating”) → mapakain (“to be able to have someone eat, to be able to feed”) mapa- + alis (“leaving”) → mapaalis (“to be able to have someone leave”); to be able to cause something to change mapa- + galing (“recovery from illness”) → mapagaling (“to be able to have someone recover or heal”) mapa- + kintab (“shine”) → mapakintab (“to be able to have something shine, to be able to make something shiny”); forms an enumerative equivalent to whether ... or ... Sasama ako sayo, mapa-Baguio, mapa-Cebu, mapa-kun'san ka man magpunta!Whether it would be Baguio, or Cebu, or anywhere you go, I would go with you! Ay jusko mare, sinasabi ko sa'yo! Mapa-selpon, mapa-kompyuter, mapa-playstation man, napakagaling maglaro ng batang 'yon.Oh, I'm telling you, sister! Whether a phone, a computer, or even a playstation, that child is so focused when it comes to gaming.

maki-.function: actor trigger - to do something involving someone else’s possessions or personal space with their permission maki- + tawag (“call”) → makitawag (“to use someone’s phone with his or her permission”) Pwede makitawag?Can I use your phone?; actor trigger - to join in an act maki- + kain (“eat”) → makikain (“to join in eating, to eat in somebody's house”) Nakikain ako kina Pepe.I ate in Pepe's house.

may-.function: prefix denoting existence or possession of something may- + akda (“literary work”) → may-akda (“author”) may- + ari (“property”) → may-ari (“owner”) may- + bahay (“house”) → maybahay (“housewife”) may- + gulang (“age”) → maygulang (“adult”) may- + lupa (“land”) → maylupa (“landowner”)

mapang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
mapam- — before initials /b/ and /p/, will sometimes remove initial /b/ and /p/
mapan- — before initials /d/, /s/, /l/ and /t/, will sometimes remove initial /d/, /s/, /l/ and /t/
From ma- + pang-.
mapang-.function: used to form adjectives: characteristic of; causing; tending to mapang- + akit → mapang-akit (“attractive”) mapang- + lustay → mapanlustay (“wasteful”)

na-.function: used to form complete aspects of verbs prefixed with ma-; (dialectal) used to form progressive aspects of verbs prefixed with um- or infixed with -um-; The dialectal prefix, when written, is generally a source of confusion and mockery for the majority of Tagalog speakers, but the two actually differ by pronunciation. The na-, where it indicates a perfective aspect, is pronounced without stress, while the na- prefix, where it indicates a progressive aspect in some dialects, is pronounced with stress. Nakain siya ng isda.He/She happened to be eaten by the fish. Nákain siya ng isda.He/She is eating fish.

nagka-.function: complete aspect of magka- Prefix for completed aspects of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to have occurred involuntarily or unexpectedly nagka- + salubong (“encounter”) → nagkasalubong (“to have met unexpectedly”) Prefix for completed aspects of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to have come to have or possess nagka- + kotse (“car”) → nagkakotse (“to have gotten a car”); Prefix for completed aspects of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to have occurred involuntarily or unexpectedly nagka- + salubong (“encounter”) → nagkasalubong (“to have met unexpectedly”); nagka- + salubong (“encounter”) → nagkasalubong (“to have met unexpectedly”); Prefix for completed aspects of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: to have come to have or possess nagka- + kotse (“car”) → nagkakotse (“to have gotten a car”); nagka- + kotse (“car”) → nagkakotse (“to have gotten a car”)

nag-.function: complete aspect of mag- Nagluto ako ng gulay. ― I have/had cooked vegetables.; (with reduplication) progressive aspect of mag- Nagluluto ako ng gulay. ― I am cooking vegetables.

nag-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
naga- — formal, literary
ga-
Compare mag-, the actor trigger prefix, and -in-, which marks the complete aspect of most verbal triggers.
nag-.function: nag- (contemplative maga-, mag-, imperative pag-); inchoative aspect of mag-; signifies that the action has started, whether or not it has been completed or still in progress Nagluto ko og utan ― I have/had cooked vegetables Nagluto ko og utan ― I am/was cooking vegetables; Some argue that the difference between the forms nag- and naga- is that of aspect and not formality, where the former is to be used as the complete aspect, and the latter as the progressive aspect. Most grammatical literature, however, does not support such aspectual distinctions, referring to these forms as variants of one another. [1] [2] This goes in line with the practical use of the prefixes.

nakiki-.function: used to form progressive aspect of verbs prefixed with maki-

nagkaka-.function: progressive aspect of magka- prefix for progressive aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: occurring involuntarily or unexpectedly nagkaka- + salubong (“encounter”) → nagkakasalubong (“meeting unexpectedly”) Prefix for progressive aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: coming to have or possess nagkaka- + kotse (“car”) → nagkakakotse (“getting a car”); prefix for progressive aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: occurring involuntarily or unexpectedly nagkaka- + salubong (“encounter”) → nagkakasalubong (“meeting unexpectedly”); Prefix for progressive aspect of verbs with actor focus and causative mood: coming to have or possess nagkaka- + kotse (“car”) → nagkakakotse (“getting a car”)

naka-.function: used to form complete aspects of verbs prefixed with maka-; in a certain position, state or condition naka- + upo (“sitting”) → nakaupo (“seated”) naka- + sulat (“writing, letter”) → nakasulat (“written”); wearing something naka- + salamin (“eyeglasses”) → nakasalamin (“wearing eyeglasses”) naka- + puti (“white”) → nakaputi (“wearing white”)

napaka-.function: forms absolute superlative forms of adjectives Napakaganda niya.She is very beautiful.

naki-.function: used to form complete aspects of verbs prefixed with maki-; Related terms[edit]; nakiki-

pa-.function: adverbial prefix - indicates process of performing the action of the verb pa- + punta (“destination”) → papunta (“be coming/ be going/ be arriving/ be on the way”) pa- + tapos (“end”) → patapos (“be about to end”) Patapos na ang pelikula.The movie is about to end.; reduced form of paki- or magpa- Patapon naman 'to sa basurahan.Could you please throw this to the trash can?; noun prefix - indicates an object that is ordered or requested to be processed according to the root word pa- + dala (“carry”) → padala (“remittance/ package [an object that is requested to be brought or sent]”) pa- + piging (“a banquet”) → papiging (“a banquet that is organized by someone [usually followed by "ng organization"or "ni name of person"]”); (informal) indication of direction Pa-Espanya ako.I'm heading for Spain.

pa-.etymology: From Proto-Philippine *pa-, from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *pa-, from Proto-Austronesian *pa-.
pa-.function: derivational verb-forming prefix to have the action of the root verb be ordered or requested pa- + saylo (“to go past something”) → pasaylo (“to ask forgiveness; to apologize”) to cause an object to have the qualities of the root adjective to involve the root noun in performing the action; to have the action of the root verb be ordered or requested pa- + saylo (“to go past something”) → pasaylo (“to ask forgiveness; to apologize”); to cause an object to have the qualities of the root adjective; to involve the root noun in performing the action; (with root reduplication) used to form verbs pretending that action pa- + patay (“dead”) → papatay-patay (“to play dead”); indication of direction pa- + ubos (“down”) → paubos (“to lower”)

nakaka-.function: causing or producing something Synonyms: ka-, nakakapang- nakaka- + takot (“fear”) → nakakatakot (“frightening”) nakaka- + inis (“annoyance”) → nakakainis (“annoying”) nakaka- + sakit (“pain”) → nakakasakit (“painful”); used to form progressive aspects of verbs prefixed with maka-; These forms are very common in use. However, in standard Tagalog, using naka- with initial reduplication of the root is more formal.; Anagrams[edit]; kaanak

nang-.function: complete aspect of mang- Nangalmot ang pusa namin. ― Our cat scratched (someone).; (with reduplication) progressive aspect of mang- Nangungulit na naman si bunso. ― The youngest is being bothersome again.

nang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
nam-
nan-
Proununciation[edit]
(Standard Cebuano)  /naŋ/ [n̪ɐŋ]
Hyphenation: nang-
Prefix[edit]
nang- (Badlit spelling ᜈᜅ᜔)
past tense of mang-
Usage notes[edit]
Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.
Derived terms[edit]
Cebuano terms prefixed with nang- nangabubho nangamote
Further reading[edit]
John U. Wolff (1972), A dictionary of Cebuano Visayan[1] (overall work in Cebuano and English), Ithaca, NY: Cornell University Press

ni-.function: alternative form of -in-; Used when ⟨l⟩, ⟨r⟩, or ⟨y⟩, is at the start of the root.

ni- (infinitive & future mo-, mu-); alternative form of ming- Nikaon ko.I ate.; The forms ming- and mi- are only ever used in formal contexts, ning- and ni- are more common in daily conversation.; See also[edit]; Cebuano terms prefixed with ni- manimaho

pag-.etymology:
From Proto-Philippine *paʀ-, from Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *paʀ-. Cognate with Cebuano pag-, and Malay per- and Indonesian per-.
From pag (“if”), which is a clipping of kapag (“if”), which is a clipping of kapagka (“if”).
pag-.function: the act of doing the action expressed by the root; or, the manner in which the action expressed by the root is performed pag- + gawa (“make”) → paggawa (“making”) pag- + basa (“read”) → pagbasa (“reading”); The root and the affixed word generally mean the same thing. Mabagal ang lakad niya. ― He/she walks slowly. Mabagal ang paglakad niya. ― He/she walks slowly.; (with syllable reduplication) the act of doing the action expressed by the root repeatedly or habitually pag- + aral (“study”) → pag-aaral (“studying habitually”) pag- + basa (“read”) → pagbabasa (“reading habitually”); when; at (or as soon as) that time that; if pag-alis nila ― when they leave/left pagbalik nila ― when they return/returned

pag-.etymology: From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *paʀ. Cognate with Malay per- and Indonesian per-, and Tagalog pag-.
pag-.function: pag-; Used to form nouns meaning "the action of (a verb)"or "the result of (a verb)"; Used to form imperatives of mo- and mag- conjugation verbs

nka-.etymology: Follows the pattern of removing unstressed vowels. Compare pki-.
nka-.function: (text messaging) abbreviation of naka- nka uniform ― wearing a uniform nkagwa knb ng assignment? ― Have you done [your] homeworks?

pagkaka-.function: act of having done the action expressed by the root; manner in which the action expressed by the root was performed; noun form of verbs beginning in magka-

paging-.etymology:
A user has added this entry to requests for verification(+) If it cannot be verified that this term meets our attestation criteria, it will be deleted. Feel free to edit this entry as normal, but do not remove {{rfv}} until the request has been resolved.
Possibly from obsolete root *ging (also possible to be *gi / *giq / *gin + -ng suffix).[1] Cognate with Cebuano pangin- and Hiligaynon pangin- and paning-. Compare maging (to be) and pagiging (being).
paging-.function: (rare) the act of change, making, or becoming towards the idea expressed by the root; or, emphasizing the fact of being or existence of the idea expressed by the root paging- + dapat (“just”) → napaging-dapat (“became justified”) paging- + dapat (“just”) → napagiging-dapat (“becoming justified”) paging- + banal (“holy”) → pinapaging-banal (“become holy; sanctified”); The root and the affixed word generally mean the same thing, although it lends greater emphasis to the fact of being or becoming expressed by the root. All known occurrences of the affix indicate it also needs a preceding prefix before it, such as ma-, pa-, i-, and kina-, amongst others. The affix is rare conversationally, and mostly found in liturgical, biblical, or philosophical use.; Ang mga pagyayaring ito ay ikinabayani ni Mabini. ― These events made Mabini into a hero.; Ang mga pangyayaring ito ay ikinapaging-bayani ni Mabini. ― These events made Mabini to become a hero.; The difference in emphasis can be discerned:; Sapagkat pinabanal ang mga ito sa pamamagitan ng salita ng Diyos at panalangin. ― Because these are made holy by the word of God and prayer.; Sapagkat pinaging-banal ang mga ito sa pamamagitan ng salita ng Diyos at panalangin. ― Because these are made to become holy by the word of God and prayer.; References[edit]; ^ Potet, Jean-Paul G. (1995), “Tagalog Monosyllabic Roots”, in Oceanic Linguistics[1], volume 34, number 2, →DOI, →ISSN, pages 345–374; Anagrams[edit]; pingga

pagkapagiging-.etymology:
A user has added this entry to requests for verification(+) If it cannot be verified that this term meets our attestation criteria, it will be deleted. Feel free to edit this entry as normal, but do not remove {{rfv}} until the request has been resolved.
From pagka- + pagiging.
pagkapagiging-.function: (rare) forms nouns from adjectives, emphasizing the state of possession of the quality expressed in the root, especially the subsistence of the essence of a thing. pagkapagiging- + wagas (“pure”) → pagkapagiging-wagas (“being pure; in the state of purity”) pagkapagiging- + tao (“human”) → pagkapagiging-tao (“being a human; humanity”)

pakikipag-.function: used to form nouns of the verbs beginning in makipag- makipag-usap (“to engage in conversation”) = pakikipag- + usap → pakikipag-usap (“engagement in conversation”)

pagka-.etymology: From pagka (“if”), which is a clipping of kapagka (“if”).
pagka-.function: forms nouns from adjectives. the state of pagka- + balisa (“anxious”) → pagkabalisa (“anxiety”); indicates posteriority in time pagka- + kain (“eat”) → pagkakain (“after eating”) Pagkakain mo, hugasan mo ang mga plato.Wash the dishes once you're done eating.; (Batangas) forms absolute superlative forms of adjectives pagka- + maganda (“beautiful”) → pagkaganda (“so beautiful”)

pala-.function: Refers to someone who frequently performs the root or is fond of performing the root pala- + biro (“joke”) → palabiro (“funny; someone who likes telling jokes”) pala- + tawa (“laugh”) → palatawa (“someone who laughs a lot”)

pagkakapaging-.etymology:
A user has added this entry to requests for verification(+) If it cannot be verified that this term meets our attestation criteria, it will be deleted. Feel free to edit this entry as normal, but do not remove {{rfv}} until the request has been resolved.
From pagkaka- + paging-.
pagkakapaging-.function: (rare) the act or manner of the act of coming to a state of being in the past of the idea expressed in the root. pagkakapaging- + hayag (“open, manifest, shown”) → pagkakapaging-hayag (“the act or manner of having come to being manifested”) pagkakapaging- + alipin (“slave”) → pagkakapaging-alipin (“the act or manner of having come to being a slave”) pagkakapaging- + maringal (“splendid”) → pagkakapaging-maringal (“the act or manner of having come to being splendid”) pagkakapaging- + guro (“teacher”) → pagkakapaging-guro (“the act or manner of having come to being a teacher”); Emphasizes a past temporal process of transition towards the realization of the idea in the root.; See also[edit]; pagkakanaging-; pagkakamaging-; pagkapagiging-

panag-.function: anything pertaining to the root panag- + araw (“sun”) → panag-araw (“anything pertaining to dry season”) panag- + ulan (“rain”) → panag-ulan (“anything pertaining to the wet season”); Further reading[edit]; “panag-”, in Pambansang Diksiyonaryo | Diksiyonaryo.ph, Manila, 2018.; Anagrams[edit]; ganap, pag- -an, panga, Gapan
plural of pag-, indicating multiple actors performing the action[1]

pakiki-.etymology: From reduplication of paki- on the second syllable.
pakiki-.function: used to form nouns referring to the habit or repetitive act of doing what the root refers to sama (“inclusion”) = pakiki- + sama → pakikisama (“act of maintaning relationships”) damay (“implication”) = pakiki- + damay → pakikiramay (“act of giving condolence”) baka (“battle of two minds”) = pakiki- + baka → pakikibaka (“act of participating in battle”) apid (“concubinage”) = pakiki- + apid → pakikiapid (“adultery; fornication”) pakiusap (“request”) > pakiki- + usap (“conversation”) → pakikiusap (“act of requesting”) pakialam (“concern”) > pakiki- + alam (“understanding”) → pakikialam (“act of meddling”)

paki-.etymology: Compare Bikol Central paki- and Kapampangan paki-. This etymology is incomplete. You can help Wiktionary by elaborating on the origins of this term.
paki-.function: adverbial prefix - Request someone to perform the action of the verb Pakitapon naman ito sa basurahan.Please throw this too to the trash can.

pampa-.function: (instrumentative prefix) forms nouns indicating agent, tool, instrument that is used to perform the action expressed by the root pampa- + lasa (“taste; flavor”) → pampalasa (“flavoring agent (such as salt, seasoning, spices)”) pampa- + alsa (“leavening”) → pampaalsa (“leaven”)

pinag-.function: attaches to a noun or a root verb which: indicates that someone willingly did it for oneself or another someone Pinagluto ko siya ng sinigang.I cooked sinigang for him. Pagod na pagod ako kahapon, kaya ginawa ko pinagpahinga ko muna sarili ko.I'm really tired yesterday, so what I did was I took a rest (for the sake of myself). indicates that someone tasked someone else to do something Pinagsulat ba naman ako ni Dwayne ng documentation naming kahaba-haba, nanakit tuloy nang sobra kamay ko!Dwayne literally made me write that super lengthy documentation of ours, it made my hand hurt so badly! Imbes na isuspinde ako dahil dun sa offense ko, pinaglinis na lang nila ako ng school grounds kada umaga nang isang buwan.Instead of me being suspended because of my offense, they simply made me clean the school grounds every morning for a month.; indicates that someone willingly did it for oneself or another someone Pinagluto ko siya ng sinigang.I cooked sinigang for him. Pagod na pagod ako kahapon, kaya ginawa ko pinagpahinga ko muna sarili ko.I'm really tired yesterday, so what I did was I took a rest (for the sake of myself).; indicates that someone tasked someone else to do something Pinagsulat ba naman ako ni Dwayne ng documentation naming kahaba-haba, nanakit tuloy nang sobra kamay ko!Dwayne literally made me write that super lengthy documentation of ours, it made my hand hurt so badly! Imbes na isuspinde ako dahil dun sa offense ko, pinaglinis na lang nila ako ng school grounds kada umaga nang isang buwan.Instead of me being suspended because of my offense, they simply made me clean the school grounds every morning for a month.; (informal, slightly vulgar, usually negative) (with duplication of first syllable of root) intensifies the verb and indicates that the effect brought by the verb was largely significant pinag- + gawa (“to do”) → pinaggagawa (“what someone did after all this time”) pinag- + taasan (“to raise the amount”) → pinagtataasan (“had raised the amount on or of every single one”) pinag- + sabi (“to say”) → pinagsasabi (“every single thing that one said”) (with triplication of first syllable of root) intensifies the verb and indicates that the effect brought by the verb is largely significant and still gives an impact up to this time being pinag- + sabi (“to say”) → pinagsasasabi (“what have someone has been saying intensively to the point it sounds nonsense”) pinag- + kana (“to attempt to do something”) → pinagkakakana (“what someone's been doing after so much going on”) pinag- + daldal (“to chat intensively”) → pinagdadadaldal (“what someone's been blabbering about”) pinag- + lampaso (“(extension) to scold someone badly”) → pinaglalalampaso (“to have each and every single one scolded as if to the point of nonstop”); (with duplication of first syllable of root) intensifies the verb and indicates that the effect brought by the verb was largely significant pinag- + gawa (“to do”) → pinaggagawa (“what someone did after all this time”) pinag- + taasan (“to raise the amount”) → pinagtataasan (“had raised the amount on or of every single one”) pinag- + sabi (“to say”) → pinagsasabi (“every single thing that one said”); (with triplication of first syllable of root) intensifies the verb and indicates that the effect brought by the verb is largely significant and still gives an impact up to this time being pinag- + sabi (“to say”) → pinagsasasabi (“what have someone has been saying intensively to the point it sounds nonsense”) pinag- + kana (“to attempt to do something”) → pinagkakakana (“what someone's been doing after so much going on”) pinag- + daldal (“to chat intensively”) → pinagdadadaldal (“what someone's been blabbering about”) pinag- + lampaso (“(extension) to scold someone badly”) → pinaglalalampaso (“to have each and every single one scolded as if to the point of nonstop”)

pinaka-.function: forms relative superlative forms of adjectives Siya ang pinakamaganda sa kanilang lahat.She is the most beautiful of them all.
pinaka-; Forms relative superlative forms of adjectives Synonym: kina- -an

pang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
pam- — before initials /b/ and /p/, will sometimes remove initial /b/ and /p/
pan- — before initials /d/, /s/, /l/ and /t/, will sometimes remove initial /d/, /s/, /l/ and /t/
From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *paŋ- (cf. Cebuano pang-, Indonesian peng- and Malay peng-).
pang-.function: (instrumentative prefix) forms nouns indicating a tool or an instrument that is used to perform the action expressed by the root pang- + tanggal (“remove, detach”) → pantanggal (“remover”) pang- + takip (“a cover”) → pantakip (“an instrument used to cover something”); (adjectival prefix) forms relational adjectives indicating use or purpose pang- + dalawa (“two”) → pandalawa (“for two”) pang- + bahay (“house”) → pambahay (“for use at home”) pang- + babae (“woman”) → pambabae (“for women”); (Ordinal prefix) Used to form ordinal numbers pang- + dalawa (“two”) → pangalawa (“second”) pang- + walo (“eight”) → pangwalo (“eighth”); Tagalog ordinal numbers prefixed with pang- are usually more informal compared to those prefixed with ika-; (with syllable reduplication) an action or a practice associated with the thing or action expressed by the root pang- + gamot (“medicine”) → panggagamot (“practice of medicine”) pang- + anak (“child”) → panganganak (“giving birth, delivery”)

pang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
panag-
From Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *paŋ- (cf. Tagalog pang-, Indonesian peng- and Malay peng-).
pang-.function: plural of pag-, indicating multiple of the action used to indicate an accustomed action or one exhibiting a system[1] used to refer to senses; used to indicate an accustomed action or one exhibiting a system[1]; used to refer to senses; derivational verb-forming prefix to refer to energetic actions or happening (verb prefix) to refer to actions of cleaning or beautifying (noun prefix) to refer to an event or place to attend or go to (adjectival prefix) to refer to becoming the qualities of the root to form verbs with no ascertainable meaning; to refer to energetic actions or happening; (verb prefix) to refer to actions of cleaning or beautifying; (noun prefix) to refer to an event or place to attend or go to; (adjectival prefix) to refer to becoming the qualities of the root; to form verbs with no ascertainable meaning; noun-forming affix to refer to performing that action (instrumentative prefix) indicates a tool or an instrument that is used to perform the action expressed by the root; to refer to performing that action; (instrumentative prefix) indicates a tool or an instrument that is used to perform the action expressed by the root; The prefix becomes pam- before ⟨b⟩ or ⟨p⟩. The consonants in question may be deleted.; The prefix becomes pan- before ⟨d⟩, ⟨l⟩, ⟨s⟩, ⟨t⟩. Where the root begins with ⟨d⟩, ⟨s⟩ or ⟨t⟩, the consonants in question may be deleted (e.g. panakot, root sakot).; Where the root begins with ⟨k⟩ or ⟨ng⟩, the consonants in question may be deleted (e.g. panguros, root krus).; Where the initial of the root is a vowel (starting with a glottal stop), the resulting affixed form is written without a hyphen (e.g. pangagad, root agad). For some, a hyphen is added after the prefix (e.g. pang-uran instead of panguran, root uran).

sang-.etymology: From Proto-Austronesian *sa (“one”, clitic form of *əsa) + Proto-Malayo-Polynesian *ŋa (linker for multiples of ten). By surface analysis, isa (“one”) + -ng. Compare Malay se-.
sang-.function: forms nouns or adjectives to denote wholeness sang- + ayon (“affirming”) → sang-ayon (“affirmative”) sang- + katutak (“multitude”) → sangkatutak (“numerous”)

sing-.function: as ... as; same as; equally Synonym: kasing- sing- + haba (“length”) → singhaba (“as long as”) Singhaba ng mesa ang lubid. ― The rope is as long as the table.

sing-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
sing-
obsolete form of hing-

sa-.function: forms terms to denote a method or means to a purpose sa- + puso (“heart”) → sapuso (“act of having put to heart”) sa- + ilalim (“below”) → sailalim (“act of undergoing”) sa- + malamig (“cold”) → samalamig (“cold beverage”) sa- + inyo (“yours”) → sainyo (“act of signing off or used to close a letter; yours truly”) sa- + makatuwid (“to be able to straighten”) → samakatwid (“therefore”) sa- + makalawa (“twice”) → samakalawa (“day after tomorrow”) sa- + una (“first”) → sauna (“of the old generation”); (may—) forms terms denoting possession of quality or characteristic sa- + pusa (“cat”) → may sa-pusa (“resembling a cat”) sa- + pagong (“turtle”) → may sa-pagong (“resembling a turtle”); (with reduplicated root, CVr-) forms terms to denote suddenness of the action: suddenly; unexpectedly; without notice sa- + basa (“read”) → sababasa (“suddenly read”) Nahihimbing ako'y sababasa siya nang malakas na aking ikinagising.I was sleeping then they suddenly read aloud that I woke up from.

tag-.function: prefix with the meaning of "time of"or "season of"tag- + ani (“harvest”) → tag-ani (“harvest season”) tag- + araw (“sun”) → tag-araw (“sunny season”) tag- + lagas (“fall”) → taglagas (“fall season”) tag- + lamig (“cold”) → taglamig (“cold season; winter”) tag- + mangga (“mango”) → tagmangga (“mango season”) tag- + ulan (“rain”) → tag-ulan (“rainy season”)

tag-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
tag-
used to denote ownership
used to a quantity or value (see usage notes)
Usage notes[edit]
The prefix is followed by a hyphen when appended to a numerical digit.

taga-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
tiga-
From Proto-Philippine *taga-. Compare Pangasinan taga.
taga-.function: prefix used to denote place or origin, birth, or residence of a person taga- + bundok (“mountain”) → tagabundok (“person from the mountains”) taga- + Maynila (“Manila”) → taga-Maynila (“person from Manila”); prefix used to denote the occupation or duty assigned to someone taga- + luto (“to cook”) → tagaluto (“cook; chef”) taga- + payo (“to advise”) → tagapayo (“adviser”) taga- + linis (“to clean”) → tagalinis (“cleaner”)

taga-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
taga-
filled to a specified extent or height

tagapag-.function: used to form agent nouns from verbs prefixed with mag-: one who does the action of the root

sari-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
saril-
Clipping of sarili.
sari-.function: self-; auto- sari- + buhay → saribuhay sari- + bilos → saribilos

tig-.etymology: Compare Cebuano tag- and Kapampangan tia-.
tig-.function: (appended to numbers) used to indicate quantity for each one or for each group tig-isaone each

tig-; (added to verbs, and sometimes nouns) used to form agent nouns, especially of a one time occasion; -er Synonyms: magCV-, mag- -Vl-, mangCV-, mang- -ay, mangCV- -ay, mang- -Vl- tigdala ― bringer tigluto ― cook tiglampaso ― scrubber (using a coconut husk)

tiga-.function: alternative form of taga-; Anagrams[edit]; igat, atig

um-.function: prefix form of -um- Umulan kanina.It rained a while ago. Umalis na ako.I already left.; Used when the root begins with a vowel. Though appearing to be a prefix, the affix actually still functions as an infix, inserted between the unwritten initial glottal stop and the root's initial vowel.; See also[edit]; Tagalog terms prefixed with um- umaa umabay umabsent umabyad umaga umagahas umagaw umagnas umakyat umalingawngaw umalis umalog umambon umamin umandar umano umanta umantabay umaapaw umapaw umaraw umasa umasim umasinta umatsoy umaawat umawat umawit umayos umebak umedad umepal umere umibig umiibig umihi umikot umiling umimik uminog uminom umiintindi umintindi umingay umiskor umisod umiwas umiyak umoo umopo umubo umuha umulan umulit umaw-aw muna umunlad umupo umurong umusbong umutot umuwi

kina- -an.function: kina- -an; Forms relative superlative forms of adjectives Synonym: pinaka- kinadak-an ― the biggest kinapusuran ― centermost portion; Stress is usually placed at the penultimate syllable of the word.; See more at ka- -an § Usage notes.

hin-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
hin-
alternative spelling of hing-

makapang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
makapam-
makapan-
From maka- + pang-.
Proununciation[edit]
(Standard Cebuano)  /makapaŋ/ [mɐ.kɐ.pɐŋ]
Hyphenation: ma‧ka‧pang-
makapang-.function: potential active of mang-; Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.

maga-.function: maga- (inchoative naga-, nag-, ga-, imperative pag-); (formal, literary) alternative form of mag-

magpang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
magpam-
magpan-
From mag- + pang-.
Proununciation[edit]
(Standard Cebuano)  /maɡpaŋ/ [mɐɡ.pɐŋ]
Hyphenation: mag‧pang-
magpang-.function: imperfective aspect of mang-; Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.

man-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
man-
alternative spelling of mang-

mam-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
mam-
alternative spelling of mang-

ming-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
ning-
mi-
ni-
Probably from a portmanteau of mu- (itself from Proto-Philippine *-um-) and the original perfect infix -in-. Compare similar developments in Ilocano -imm-, and archaic Tagalog -ungm-.
ming-.function: ming- (infinitive & future mo-, mu-); complete aspect of mo- Mingkaon ko.I ate.; The forms ming- and mi- are only ever used in formal contexts, ning- and ni- are more common in daily conversation.; Related terms[edit]; gi-gina--in-; See also[edit]; Category Cebuano terms prefixed with ming- not found

mo-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
mu-
Standardized form of mu-, from Proto-Austronesian *mu- (movement prefix).
mo-.function: mo- (inchoative ming-, mi-, ni-, contemplative mo-); actor trigger verb prefix, infinitive form non-durative action, an action done without reference to duration; see Perfective aspect Coordinate term: mag- — durative agent trigger Mokaon ko.I eat. (I is focused.) volitional action Wala siya mosultiHe wouldn't say/refused to say; non-durative action, an action done without reference to duration; see Perfective aspect Coordinate term: mag- — durative agent trigger Mokaon ko.I eat. (I is focused.); volitional action Wala siya mosultiHe wouldn't say/refused to say; contemplative aspect of mo- Mokaon ko.I will eat.

mang- -ay.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
mam- -ay
man- -ay
Prefix[edit]
mang- -ay (Badlit spelling ᜋᜅ᜔ ᜀᜌ᜔) (regional, with initial syllable repetition)
alternative form of mang-
forms nouns signifying occupations or doer of the action expressed by the root
Usage notes[edit]
Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.
Derived terms[edit]
Category Cebuano terms prefixed with mang- -ay not found
Further reading[edit]
John U. Wolff (1972), A dictionary of Cebuano Visayan[1] (overall work in Cebuano and English), Ithaca, NY: Cornell University Press

nagpang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
nagpam-
nagpan-
From nag- + pang-.
Proununciation[edit]
(Standard Cebuano)  /naɡpaŋ/ [n̪ɐɡ.pɐŋ]
Hyphenation: nag‧pang-
nagpang-.function: inchoative aspect of magpang-; Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.

mu-.function: mu- (perfect ming-, mi-, ni-); alternative form of mo-; See also[edit]; Category Cebuano terms prefixed with mu- not found

nakapang-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
nakapam-
nakapan-
From naka- + pang-.
Proununciation[edit]
(Standard Cebuano)  /nakapaŋ/ [n̪ɐ.kɐ.pɐŋ]
Hyphenation: na‧ka‧pang-
nakapang-.function: inchoative aspect of makapang-; Along with the prefix pang-, this prefix is affected by initial consonant mutations.

naga-.etymology:
Alternative forms[edit]
ga- — short form, common
Compare nag-.
naga-.function: naga- (contemplative maga-, mag-, imperative pag-); (formal, literary) alternative form of nag- Nagatuo ako sa Diyos Amahan, Makagagahom sa tanan...I believe in God, the Father Almighty...; Some argue that the difference between the forms nag- and naga- is that of aspect and not formality, where the former is to be used as the complete aspect, and the latter as the progressive aspect. Most grammatical literature, however, does not support such aspectual distinctions, referring to these forms as variants of one another. [1] [2] This goes in line with the practical use of the prefixes.; References[edit]; ^ Manuel Yap (1947), Ang Dila Natong Bisaya[1] (overall work in Cebuano), page 135 ^ Fr. Juan Felis de la Encarnación (1851), Diccionario bisaya-español[2] (overall work in Cebuano and Spanish), Amigos del País, page 225

pan-.etymology:
Prefix[edit]
pan- (Badlit spelling ᜉᜈ᜔)
alternative form of pang-
Derived terms[edit]
Cebuano terms prefixed with pan- pangang-ang panagat panginahanglan pagpanira pagpanunggab panagang paningsingan paniper panaghigala panaguto panawagan paningog panitik panuhod panultol

ning-.function: ning- (infinitive & future mo-, mu-); alternative form of ming- Ningkaon ko.I ate.; The forms ming- and mi- are primarily used in formal contexts (e.g. news reporting), with ning- and ni- are more common in daily conversation.
